title: Dracaena neoserrulata
taxonomy: Dracaena neoserrulata belongs to the family Asparagaceae, which is a group of flowering plants that have been reclassified from the previous family Dracaenaceae. It is a part of the genus Dracaena, which comprises over 40 species of plants native to tropical Africa and Asia. Dracaena neoserrulata is a specific species within this genus, characterized by its unique foliage and growth habits. The species is often used as an ornamental plant in indoor and outdoor settings due to its adaptability and ability to thrive in various environments. In terms of its classification, Dracaena neoserrulata is a member of the order Asparagales.

intro: Dracaena neoserrulata is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae, commonly known as a type of dragon tree or red-leafed dragon tree due to its striking red-tipped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which have been shown to help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Dracaena neoserrulata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Dracaena neoserrulata is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly prepared to treat respiratory infections and other health conditions.
